mission: Create one canonical NHS login partner-onboarding rehearsal and gated live-submission pack so Vecells can exercise product-fit, sandpit readiness, demo readiness, evidence readiness, and later real-provider submission without implying current live eligibility.

official_guidance.title: Apply for NHS login
official_guidance.summary: Application stage checks eligibility, commissioner or sponsor posture, IM1 or PDS linked dependencies, and board approval before any later integration work.
grounding: Service must be commissioned or sponsored by an NHS organisation or local authority.
NHS login does not cover clinical authorisation.
Board approval of the use case does not itself mean live approval.

official_guidance.title: Discovery
official_guidance.summary: Discovery requires a sandpit proof of concept, forms-and-documents review, product demonstration checklist completion, and current architecture, data-flow, and user-journey artefacts.
grounding: Discovery expects a proof of concept in sandpit before preparation and product demonstration calls.
The product demonstration pack must include architecture, data-flow, and user-journey artefacts.
The discovery demo must show allow and deny share cases, repeat sign-in, settings navigation, and P5 or P9 uplift where applicable.

official_guidance.title: Integrate
official_guidance.summary: Integration starts only after the product demonstration call, then runs through integration environment access, SCAL and clinical-safety evidence, connection agreement, and National Service Desk registration.
grounding: Integration environment access comes only after the Product Demonstration Call.
SCAL, DSPT, privacy notice, clinical safety case, hazard log, and DCB0129 or DCB0160 posture are explicit evidence requirements.
Connection agreement and National Service Desk registration are mandatory before go live.

official_guidance.title: How NHS login works
official_guidance.summary: NHS login authenticates and verifies the user, but partner services own session management, logout, product authorization, age controls, and least-necessary scope requests.
grounding: User agreement to share data is required and consent denial must be handled explicitly.
Partner services remain responsible for session management and logout.
NHS login is not a one-time GP credential retrieval tool and does not replace PDS.

official_guidance.title: How do I integrate to the sandpit?
official_guidance.summary: Sandpit setup requires a friendly service name, redirect URI, public key, and scope list. Sandpit is suitable for proof of concept and login flow rehearsal, not full assurance or live approval.
grounding: Required information includes friendly name, redirect URI, public key, and requested scopes.
Sandpit does not support real identity documents and does not usually approve new registrations.
Prepared test data and login flow exercises come after the sandpit request is processed.

official_guidance.title: Compare NHS login environments
official_guidance.summary: Sandpit has no formal requirements, integration requires sandpit completion and DSPT or ODS posture, and live production requires completed readiness activity plus signed agreement.
grounding: Sandpit supports proof of concept and login journey exploration only.
Integration environment requires sandpit completion plus ODS code and DSPT covering the service scope.
Live production requires readiness activity and a signed agreement.

official_guidance.title: Multiple redirect URIs
official_guidance.summary: NHS login supports up to 10 redirect URIs and advises state-based fan-out when more are needed.
grounding: Use the opaque state parameter to preserve partner-side routing context.
NHS login supports at most 10 redirect URIs.
Redirect inventory is an operational governance topic, not a form-only detail.

fields.label: Service name
fields.mock_value: Vecells
fields.actual_placeholder: Partner-facing product name exactly as submitted in application and sandpit forms.

fields.label: Service summary
fields.mock_value: FHIR-native primary care access and operations layer that routes patient demand into one safety-gated request pipeline.
fields.actual_placeholder: Public-facing product summary used in the application and product demo.

fields.label: Patient eligibility statement
fields.mock_value: Vecells serves patients registered at GP practices in England or receiving NHS services in England.
fields.actual_placeholder: Exact wording used to justify NHS login eligibility.

fields.label: Commissioner or sponsor posture
fields.mock_value: ASSUMPTION_NHS_LOGIN_SPONSOR_PLACEHOLDER: commissioner and sponsor are not yet named; current pack preserves placeholder-only governance and blocks live progression.
fields.actual_placeholder: Named commissioner, NHS sponsor, or explicit commissioning discussion status.

fields.label: Sponsor contact placeholder
fields.mock_value: TBD sponsor owner - blocked until NHS commissioner or sponsor is confirmed.
fields.actual_placeholder: Named sponsor or commissioner contact.

fields.label: Patient route families
fields.mock_value: Public request intake, authenticated status tracking, request claim and resume, booking manage, pharmacy follow-up, and conversation or callback follow-up.
fields.actual_placeholder: Route-family inventory exactly mapped to user journeys shown in demo and dossier.

fields.label: Identity and session boundary
fields.mock_value: NHS login authenticates the patient and returns claims. Vecells still decides local session creation, route intent, access grant redemption, writable authority, and same-shell recovery.
fields.actual_placeholder: Production wording for partner onboarding and architecture review.

fields.label: User journey inventory
fields.mock_value: Registration allow-share, registration deny-share, repeat sign-in, settings-link return, P5 navigation, P9 uplift, and delegated-access placeholder.
fields.actual_placeholder: Current product journey inventory used in product demo and SCAL artefacts.

fields.label: Architecture diagram narrative
fields.mock_value: Client-first React shell, gateway-managed browser boundary, dual-UK-region runtime, identity bridge, event spine, audit and release parity controls.
fields.actual_placeholder: Updated architecture narrative used with the submitted diagram.

fields.label: Data flow diagram narrative
fields.mock_value: Claims from NHS login enter the identity bridge, settle into local session and route-intent decisions, then drive bounded patient actions and downstream notification, booking, or pharmacy adapters.
fields.actual_placeholder: Updated data-flow narrative used with the submitted diagram.

fields.label: Demo attendees
fields.mock_value: Product lead, technical architect, security lead, privacy lead, clinical safety owner, and sponsor placeholder.
fields.actual_placeholder: Named attendees for the preparation and product demonstration calls.

fields.label: Demo walkthrough script
fields.mock_value: 24_demo_walkthrough_internal_v1
fields.actual_placeholder: Current demo script or rehearsal recording reference.

fields.label: Sandpit friendly service name
fields.mock_value: Vecells Partner Access Atelier
fields.actual_placeholder: Exact friendly name for the sandpit environment request form.

fields.label: Primary redirect URI
fields.mock_value: https://vecells.example/mock/callback/nhs-login
fields.actual_placeholder: Current callback URI registered for the chosen environment.

fields.label: Public key bundle reference
fields.mock_value: PUBKEY_VECELLS_NHS_LOGIN_NONPROD_01
fields.actual_placeholder: Public key bundle and custody reference to submit.

fields.label: Scopes and claims summary
fields.mock_value: openid profile email phone profile_extended (placeholder only), with route-family checks enforcing least-necessary use and local authorization after callback.
fields.actual_placeholder: Current scope and claims request matrix by route family.

fields.label: Vector of trust posture
fields.mock_value: P5 for low-risk signed-in portal continuity and P9 for PHI-bearing request ownership, booking manage, and pharmacy follow-up; final route matrix remains governed by local capability and session law.
fields.actual_placeholder: Final VoT matrix agreed during product demonstration.

fields.label: Indicative go-live target
fields.mock_value: ASSUMPTION_NHS_LOGIN_GO_LIVE_WINDOW: no live date is proposed until sponsor, MVP, and approvals exist.
fields.actual_placeholder: Nominal target date agreed after product demo.

fields.label: DSPT status
fields.mock_value: Not yet published for live scope; mock dossier tracks placeholder only.
fields.actual_placeholder: In-year DSPT status covering NHS login service scope.

fields.label: Privacy notice reference
fields.mock_value: PRIVACY_NOTICE_VECELLS_NHS_LOGIN_DRAFT
fields.actual_placeholder: Published NHS login-compliant privacy notice reference.

fields.label: Clinical safety owner
fields.mock_value: ROLE_MANUFACTURER_CSO placeholder - evidence bundle not yet signed.
fields.actual_placeholder: Named Clinical Safety Officer and supporting sign-off references.

fields.label: Connection agreement signatory
fields.mock_value: TBD signatory - blocked until commissioner or product ownership model is confirmed.
fields.actual_placeholder: Named signatory or commissioner-owner for the connection agreement.

fields.label: Service desk registration contacts
fields.mock_value: Operations lead, support lead, incident manager, and out-of-hours escalation placeholders only.
fields.actual_placeholder: Current Service Bridge registration contacts and escalation details.

fields.label: Named approver
fields.actual_placeholder: Named approver required before any live portal mutation.

fields.label: Environment target
fields.actual_placeholder: sandpit | integration | production

fields.label: Live mutation flag
fields.mock_value: false
fields.actual_placeholder: ALLOW_REAL_PROVIDER_MUTATION=true

artifacts.name: Live selector map and dry-run harness

manual_checkpoints.label: Internal eligibility review
rejection_reasons: Service is not commissioned or sponsored.
Route logic suggests NHS login is being treated as authorization.
Commissioner or sponsor posture is still unknown.

manual_checkpoints.label: Preparation call readiness
rejection_reasons: Architecture or data-flow artefacts are stale.
User journeys do not include consent-denied or repeat sign-in flows.
Demo attendees do not cover technical, privacy, and clinical safety responsibilities.

manual_checkpoints.label: Product demonstration outcome
rejection_reasons: Product demo has not happened yet.
Vector of Trust or scopes remain unresolved.
Commercial, legal, or AI questions remain open.

manual_checkpoints.label: Security and privacy review
rejection_reasons: Privacy notice is missing or not NHS login compliant.
Evidence pack is stale or not scoped to the NHS login feature.
Data-transfer and hosting posture are unclear.

manual_checkpoints.label: Clinical safety review
rejection_reasons: Clinical safety case is missing.
Hazard log is missing or unsigned.
Medical device assessment is not current.

manual_checkpoints.label: Connection agreement and special terms review
rejection_reasons: Signatory is not confirmed.
Commissioner ownership model remains unclear.
Special terms or GDPR relationship questions are unresolved.

manual_checkpoints.label: Signatory confirmed
rejection_reasons: No legal signatory has been identified.
Commissioner ownership model is still placeholder-only.

manual_checkpoints.label: Service desk registration review
rejection_reasons: No named incident contacts or escalation path exist.
Service Bridge handoff details are incomplete.

manual_checkpoints.label: Pause-and-confirm before final submission
rejection_reasons: ALLOW_REAL_PROVIDER_MUTATION is not enabled.
Named approver, target environment, or current evidence bundle is missing.
Phase 0 external-readiness gate is still withheld.

stages.label: Application draft
stages.summary: Capture product summary, eligibility, sponsor placeholder, and basic service facts.

stages.label: Product fit review
stages.summary: Lock the route-family description and the boundary that NHS login authenticates but does not authorize.

stages.label: Demo prep
stages.summary: Prepare the current architecture, data flow, user journeys, and live-demo walkthrough.

stages.label: Sandpit request ready
stages.summary: Prepare the friendly name, redirect URI, public key bundle, scope list, and environment rationale.

stages.label: Sandpit requested
stages.summary: Record the sandpit request and treat the account pack as pending until the provider responds.

stages.label: Product demo pending
stages.summary: Stage the proof-of-concept demo, the sponsor placeholder, and the walkthrough pack.

stages.label: Integration request blocked until demo
stages.summary: Make the provider rule explicit: integration access follows the product demonstration, not the other way around.

stages.label: Integration request ready
stages.summary: After demo approval, package the integration request and nominal target date with current redirect and key material.

stages.label: Assurance bundle in progress
stages.summary: Prepare SCAL, DSPT, privacy, safety, and architecture evidence without implying that any signed approval exists yet.

stages.label: Connection agreement pending
stages.summary: Separate the legal signatory path from technical readiness and keep ownership questions explicit.

stages.label: Service desk registration pending
stages.summary: Prepare the Service Bridge contacts and incident posture after connection-agreement work is current.

stages.label: Ready for real submission
stages.summary: Mock rehearsal can reach this dossier-complete state, but real submission remains fail-closed until the live gates pass.

live_gates.label: Phase 0 external-readiness gate
live_gates.summary: seq_020 still reports `withheld` for Phase 0 entry; no real provider mutation may proceed while the external-readiness chain remains withheld.

live_gates.label: Credible MVP or demo environment
live_gates.summary: The rehearsal environment exists, but there is not yet a real sponsor-approved MVP or live-admissible demo environment.

live_gates.label: Sponsor or commissioner identified
live_gates.summary: The current dossier intentionally preserves sponsor and commissioner placeholders instead of inventing names.

live_gates.label: Architecture and data-flow artefacts current
live_gates.summary: This task creates the current architecture narrative, data-flow narrative, and the submission checklist rows that keep them current.

live_gates.label: User journeys current
live_gates.summary: The application field map and demo pack keep the required allow-share, deny-share, repeat sign-in, settings, and uplift flows explicit.

live_gates.label: Safety and privacy bundle current
live_gates.summary: The pack seeds the required bundle, but live submission remains blocked until those artefacts become current and signed.

live_gates.label: Named approver present
live_gates.summary: No named live approver is stored in repo fixtures or synthetic seed data.

live_gates.label: Environment target present
live_gates.summary: Real runs must specify sandpit, integration, or production as a named target. The pack intentionally leaves the field blank.

live_gates.label: Live mutation flag enabled
live_gates.summary: All generated browser automation defaults to dry-run and refuses live submission until ALLOW_REAL_PROVIDER_MUTATION=true.

draft_values.fld_service_summary: FHIR-native primary care access and operations layer for safe access, routing, booking, pharmacy, and communications continuity.
draft_values.fld_patient_eligibility: Patients registered at GP practices in England or receiving NHS services in England.
draft_values.fld_commissioner_posture: Sponsor and commissioner still placeholder-only; live progression blocked until real ownership exists.
draft_values.fld_route_families: public_request, authenticated_status, request_claim, booking_manage, pharmacy_follow_up, communications
draft_values.fld_identity_boundary: Authentication and consent share happen via NHS login; local session, route intent, grants, and writable authority stay inside Vecells.
draft_values.fld_user_journeys: allow_share, deny_share, repeat_sign_in, settings_return, p5_navigation, p9_uplift, delegated_access_placeholder
draft_values.fld_architecture_summary: Client-first browser shell, gateway boundary, identity bridge, audit, and dual-UK-region runtime.
draft_values.fld_data_flow_summary: Claims enter bridge, settle session and route intent, and only then unlock bounded patient actions.
draft_values.fld_demo_team: product-lead; technical-architect; security-lead; privacy-lead; clinical-safety-owner; sponsor-placeholder
draft_values.fld_vector_of_trust_profile: P5 for continuity, P9 for PHI-bearing actions
draft_values.fld_integration_target_date: No live date until approvals exist

assumptions.summary: Current-stage Vecells does not yet have a named NHS commissioner or sponsor in repo-backed evidence, so the pack keeps the field explicit and blocks live progression.
The real target date remains blank until product demonstration, sponsor, and assurance evidence become real rather than synthetic.
